flashcards.back: ASR is the computational task of converting spoken language into written text. It involves acoustic signal processing, phonetic modeling, and language understanding to transcribe audio input into sequential text output.

flashcards.front: What is an Acoustic Model in ASR?
flashcards.back: An acoustic model maps audio features (MFCCs, spectrograms, or mel-filterbanks) to phonemes or subword units. Deep neural networks (DNNs, CNNs, LSTMs) or transformers are commonly used to model the relationship between acoustic signals and phonetic representations.

flashcards.front: What is a Language Model (LM) in ASR?
flashcards.back: A language model computes the probability of a word sequence P(w₁,w₂,...,wₙ). It helps resolve acoustic ambiguities by preferring grammatically correct and contextually probable text outputs. N-gram models and neural LMs (like RNN-LM) are commonly used.

CTC is a loss function that enables training of sequence-to-sequence models without explicit alignment between input audio and output transcript. It introduces a 'blank' token to handle variable-length alignments and collapses repeated tokens to produce the final output.

flashcards.front: What is the Encoder-Decoder Architecture in End-to-End ASR?
flashcards.back: The encoder (typically LSTM, Transformer, or Conformer) processes audio features into a high-level representation. The decoder (attention-based) then generates text tokens auto-regressively, using attention to align acoustic features with predicted output tokens.

flashcards.front: What is Beam Search Decoding in ASR?
flashcards.back: Beam search is a decoding algorithm that maintains the top-k most probable hypotheses at each time step instead of greedy decoding (best path). It balances computational efficiency with accuracy by exploring multiple partial sequences and selecting the best final transcript.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between CTC and RNN-Transducer (RNN-T)?
flashcards.back: CTC assumes output tokens are conditionally independent given input, while RNN-T learns a joint probability of audio and output sequences through a separate prediction network. RNN-T handles streaming better and models output dependencies, making it more suitable for real-time ASR.

flashcards.front: What is a Conformer Architecture in Modern ASR?
flashcards.back: The Conformer combines self-attention (for global context) and convolution (for local feature extraction) in alternating layers. This hybrid design captures both long-range dependencies and fine-grained local patterns, achieving state-of-the-art results in end-to-end ASR systems.
